notes: Two user turns in ONE live opencode `--session <id>` chain, separated by a MID-SESSION model swap: turn 1 runs on model A (config default lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored), then turn 2 runs on model B (lmstudio/qwen/qwen3-4b) via the `send` step's `model` field threading `opencode run -m` — the gap:model-select unblock (driver commit bbf82830). THE SWITCH IS EVIDENCED BY THE TRANSCRIPT, NOT THE GOLDEN: transcript.jsonl carries the per-turn `_model` (turn-1 user part = gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored, turn-2 user part = qwen/qwen3-4b), which is the recorded proof the driver ran turn 2 on a different model on the same --session chain. It is NOT surfaced in an automated assertion: summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden is EMPTY (omitted) because the replay metrics reader looks for the model on the assistant/step-finish parts where opencode's `_model` is absent — the same model-identification replay gap (model name not re-derivable in the replay golden); events.jsonl carries no model field either. So the A→B switch is verified by inspecting the transcript `_model` values, while this spec asserts the structural lifecycle around it. KNOWN_FAILING — turn2_done (the model-B turn's FINAL working→ready settle) is DELIBERATELY NOT ASSERTED. On the headless per-turn `opencode run` driver the final turn's subprocess EXITS ~140ms after the daemon observes working, inside the settle debounce, so the terminal step-finish→ready lands after process_exited and is missed (the settle-after-exit race — the SAME root cause that made long-agentic-session-stress / multiple-agents-same-workspace known_failing before run_live). run_live (one long-lived process) would capture the final settle, but it drives the TUI and cannot do the deterministic per-turn `-m` switch this cell requires, so model-switch stays headless and accepts the un-observable final settle (maintainer decision 2026-05-29). Verified live 2026-05-29: turn 1 (gemma-4-e2b) settled ready 61ms before its process exit; turn 2 (qwen/qwen3-4b) completed in the store (step-finish reason=stop) but process_exited fired 141ms after working with no working→ready emitted. WHY qwen3-4b as model B: 26B/30B-class local models are too slow prefilling opencode's ~22k-token turn-2 context (>300s, timed out); functiongemma-270m has a broken chat template; per issue #440 model B need only be a DIFFERENT reachable model, so a small fast template-compatible model is used locally. SESSION IDENTITY (opencode-specific): opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter — session state lives in the SQLite store and the daemon observes a UUID-keyed ses_… session; the driver chains turn 2 with `--session <id>` so both turns share one session record. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity; turn1_start establishes the session identity later phases pin to via same_session_as. HOW runs differ: concrete model values are environment-dependent (issue #440 — assert presence/change, not a hardcoded pair); assistant wording, per-turn token counts, the ses_… UUID/PID, and sub-second timing vary. Stable structurally: turn 1 ready→working→ready on model A, then the SAME ses_… enters working again on model B (turn2_start), no waiting ever.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch (the ses_… store session); never enters waiting (no tool use)
PID bound to the ses_… session
First prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working on model A
First turn completes back to ready on model A, same ses_… session; state never entered waiting

After the mid-session model switch, the second prompt drives the SAME ses_… session ready → working, now on model B (transcript _model = qwen/qwen3-4b) — proving a real second turn on a different model on the same --session chain. turn2_done (the model-B turn's final settle to ready) is known_failing on the headless driver — see meta notes (settle-after-exit race).
